canto_title: The Flight from the Malebranche and the Encounter with the Hypocrites

scenes.scene_name: The Fable of the Frog and the Mouse
scenes.summary: Dante reflects on an Aesop's fable to process his spiraling thoughts and growing fear.
Dante expresses his fear of the Malebranche demons approaching them from behind.
Virgil reassures Dante and proposes a plan to navigate to the next bolgia to avoid the hunt.
As the demons approach, Virgil quickly carries Dante down the steep, rocky path like a parent with a child.
The travelers reach the bottom of the pit, where Divine Providence has placed the ministers of the fifth ditch.
Dante observes the hypocrites wearing heavy, gold-plated yet lead-filled cloaks.
The travelers watch the exhausted, slow-moving crowd of hypocrites walking in sorrow.
Dante asks Virgil to keep his eyes moving to identify the inhabitants of this circle.
A voice from the group of hypocrites calls out to the fleeing travelers.
Virgil instructs Dante to wait for the speaker to approach.
Two hypocrites hurry toward Dante and Virgil, though they are slowed by their heavy burdens.
The hypocrites stare at Dante, wondering how a living man could be walking among the dead.
The hypocrites mockingly welcome Dante to the college of the sorrowful hypocrites.
Dante introduces himself and asks the hypocrites about their intense suffering.
A hypocrite explains the weight of their leaden cloaks and identifies themselves as various clerics.
Dante's attempt to speak is interrupted by the sight of a crucifix on the ground, which causes a monk to react visibly.
A Catalan monk explains that the crucifix serves as a reminder of the betrayal of Christ by the Pharisees.
Virgil expresses his dismay at the sight of the crucifix so cruelly placed in the pit.
Virgil asks if there is any opening or tool nearby that might allow them to escape the black angels.
The monk informs Virgil that a massive, moving stone blocks their path and covers the exit.
Virgil laments that the demon's calculation of the sinners' number was incorrect.
A monk adds that the devil is known in Bologna as the father of lies.
Irritated, Virgil leads Dante away from the heavy-laden figures toward the edge of the plants.
